tldr: Add Avo for tracking plan governance; PostHog often stays as the destination since these tools complement each other.
why_switch: Avo enforces a defined tracking plan to reduce schema drift
Better data quality before events hit PostHog (or any destination)
Type-safe event firing via Avo's Codegen reduces instrumentation bugs
Centralized event documentation owned by product + engineering
Realistically: this is often a complement to PostHog, not a replacement
what_to_export: List of events and their properties from PostHog
Saved insights, funnels, paths, retention (for reference)
Feature flags and experiments (stay in PostHog)
export_method: PostHog supports SQL/HogQL access to events and a project export option; pull the event and property schema using whichever is current per the official PostHog docs. For property and event documentation, capture names, types, and descriptions as the input to Avo's plan.
import_method: Create an Avo workspace, define the tracking plan from the PostHog schema, and connect Avo's destination integration to PostHog (or whatever destination you use). Integrate Avo Inspector/Codegen into the codebase per the official Avo docs to enforce the plan at instrumentation time.
gotchas: Avo is governance, not a PostHog replacement — usually you keep both
Don't double-instrument; route everything through Avo to PostHog
Existing PostHog events with messy names will need normalization
Engineering must adopt Codegen/Inspector for the plan to actually be enforced
Confirm Avo's PostHog destination integration is current per Avo's docs
Communicate that PostHog isn't going away if teams worry about losing replays/flags

email.subject: Adding Avo on top of PostHog for event governance
email.body:
Team,

We're adding Avo as our tracking plan governance layer. PostHog stays — it remains our analytics destination, with feature flags and session replay continuing there.

Avo will enforce event names, properties, and types before events reach PostHog, cutting down on schema drift. I'll export the current PostHog schema, draft an initial Avo plan, and circulate for review. Engineering will then integrate Avo's Codegen.

Flag any concerns about workflow changes.

Thanks
